pages.title: About - Darren Paffey MP
pages.meta_description: Your local voice for Southampton Itchen
pages.text_excerpt: About - Darren Paffey MP Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About My story My Missions for Southampton Itchen. My Campaigns Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Bring a Neighbourhood Health Centre to Bitterne Bring Back Local Banking! Step-Free Access at Woolston Train Station More Bobbies on the Beat End the Cladding & Leasehold Scandal Bathing Water Status for the River Itchen Latest News Parliamentary Activity Get in Touch Contact Me Invite Darren to Your Event Upcoming Events SEND Parent & Carer Coffee Morning Town Hall Q&A with Darren Paffey MP Advice Surgeries Pint with your MP Southampton Action Forums Newsletter Sign Up More Search Search the website Search Menu About I’m delighted to have been elected to parliament as the Labour MP for Southampton Itchen. The country has voted for change, and I am proud to be part of the government that will take the tough decisions that the last government dodged, in order to bring about the national renewal that we all know is needed. I got into politics to work together with communities to make Southampton a better place to be, and before I became an MP, I was a university lecturer and served as a city councillor since 2011. I’ve also served as the Cabinet Member for Children and Learning, and was Deputy Leader of Southampton City Council. My Story Darren Paffey "Breaking down barriers to opportunity for all and unlocking Southampton's true potential is my driving mission" - Darren Paffey MP for Southampton Itchen My Maiden Speech in Parliament Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Darren Paffey MP at PO Box 2002, Southampton, SO18 9TQ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.meta_description: Your local voice for Southampton Itchen
pages.text_excerpt: My Missions for Southampton Itchen. - Darren Paffey MP Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About My story My Missions for Southampton Itchen. My Campaigns Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Bring a Neighbourhood Health Centre to Bitterne Bring Back Local Banking! Step-Free Access at Woolston Train Station More Bobbies on the Beat End the Cladding & Leasehold Scandal Bathing Water Status for the River Itchen Latest News Parliamentary Activity Get in Touch Contact Me Invite Darren to Your Event Upcoming Events SEND Parent & Carer Coffee Morning Town Hall Q&A with Darren Paffey MP Advice Surgeries Pint with your MP Southampton Action Forums Newsletter Sign Up More Search Search the website Search Menu My Missions for Southampton Itchen. Mission One: Supporting local people with the cost of living. Mission Two: Reduce crime in our city with community officers on our streets. Mission Three: Make our local NHS fit for the future - so you can see a GP within 48 hours. Mission Four: Help Southampton schools get the investment they need. Mission Five: Clean up the river itchen from pollution and protect the environment. pages.title: Have your say on £20m funding for Weston - Darren Paffey MP
pages.h1: Have your say on £20m funding for Weston
pages.meta_description: Your local voice for Southampton Itchen
headings: h2: Have your say on £20m funding for Weston
pages.text_excerpt: Have your say on £20m funding for Weston - Darren Paffey MP Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About My story My Missions for Southampton Itchen. My Campaigns Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Bring a Neighbourhood Health Centre to Bitterne Bring Back Local Banking! Step-Free Access at Woolston Train Station More Bobbies on the Beat End the Cladding & Leasehold Scandal Bathing Water Status for the River Itchen Latest News Parliamentary Activity Get in Touch Contact Me Invite Darren to Your Event Upcoming Events SEND Parent & Carer Coffee Morning Town Hall Q&A with Darren Paffey MP Advice Surgeries Pint with your MP Southampton Action Forums Newsletter Sign Up More Search Search the website Search Menu Have your say on £20m funding for Weston As you may have seen last week, I am delighted that the government has confirmed that that we have secured £20 million funding for Weston though the government’s Plan for Neighbourhoods. That’s going to amount to £2 million funding every year for Weston, particularly the areas concentrated around the Weston housing estates, for the next ten years. It’s not going to be for politicians in London to decide how exactly this money is spent. Local people will play a key role in deciding how it’s used. To kick-start this process, I’m asking residents and businesses in Weston to tell me what matters most to them – and how they think this once-in-a-generation funding should be used. This is just the first step in this exciting journey. In time. the fund will rely on local people coming together to decide how to spend the money, with decision-making put in the hands of an independent board of the MP, local councillors, community leaders and residents. Please fill in the survey below. By speaking up together, we can make sure this funding delivers for every part of our community in Weston. Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Name (Required) First Last Email Address (Required) First Line of Address Postcode (Required) What do you enjoy or value most about living in Weston? Which of the following local issues or challenges matter to you most? Please select up to three. Local roads and footpaths Crime and anti-social behaviour Public transport Community centres and services Playgrounds and green spaces Health and care services Housing and regeneration Education and employment support Culture and leisure facilities Don't worry if your issue isn't listed - you can add your own next. If you had to choose, what's the one project or improvement that you would like to see happen in Weston? (Required) Are there any key people or local groups already doing good work here? Please name them if you can - we'll reach out to them too. Would you be interested in helping to shape or deliver community projects? (Required) Yes please No thanks If you've ticked yes, you agree that we can contact you in future to let you know about suitable opportunities to be involved in this work. Any other thoughts you'd like to share on this funding opportunity or about improvements to Weston? Please tick if you would you like to subscribe to my newsletter and keep to date with what's happening in Weston and across Southampton Itchen? Please tick if you'd like to be kept up to date Darren Paffey MP and the Labour Party will use the personal information you provided for the purpose of the Your Say Weston campaign and Darren’s newsletter. We may also contact you by post using the electoral register, which Darren is entitled to use as an MP and the Labour Party is entitled to use to further its objectives as a political party. You can opt out of communications from us or manage your preferences at any time. For more information about how we use personal data please visit our Privacy Policy. Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Darren Paffey MP at PO Box 2002, Southampton, SO18 9TQ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.text_excerpt: Advice Surgeries - Darren Paffey MP Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About My story My Missions for Southampton Itchen. My Campaigns Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Bring a Neighbourhood Health Centre to Bitterne Bring Back Local Banking! Step-Free Access at Woolston Train Station More Bobbies on the Beat End the Cladding & Leasehold Scandal Bathing Water Status for the River Itchen Latest News Parliamentary Activity Get in Touch Contact Me Invite Darren to Your Event Upcoming Events SEND Parent & Carer Coffee Morning Town Hall Q&A with Darren Paffey MP Advice Surgeries Pint with your MP Southampton Action Forums Newsletter Sign Up More Search Search the website Search Menu Advice Surgeries Advice Surgeries Darren Paffey MP at his first surgery in Bitterne Library Darren regularly hosts monthly in-person surgeries for all residents in the constituency. These are held usually during the day on a Friday or a Saturday at local community facilities, moving between Bitterne, the City Centre and Woolston locations. Attendance to these surgeries is by booked 15-minute appointment only. To book an appointment please call the office on 02380 017393 or email the office at [email protected] . Appointments are offered on a first come, first served basis. When booking please provide: your name, contact details, address, and preferred time Upcoming surgeries are listed below. If you don’t see a date or location that suits you, please check back soon as this page will be updated frequently with details of upcoming surgeries. 27th February 2026 - Bitterne Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Darren Paffey MP at PO Box 2002, Southampton, SO18 9TQ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
